subtitle: AI-Powered Customer Engagement Platforms
hero_sub: 15+ years leading technical onboarding, requirements discovery, and platform configuration for SaaS and AI-powered solutions — with direct experience integrating CRMs, APIs, and cloud platforms for distributed, multi-country client organizations. I own the implementation lifecycle from first discovery call to measurable time-to-value.

the_case.need: 4+ years hands-on technical role; 1+ years delivering customer implementations
the_case.answer: 15+ years of client-facing technical delivery: Understood.org (CRM integration + replatform implementation), Eptura (SaaS platform adoption across ~10 teams), Crocs (technical discovery + workflow configuration), OVRSTCH (founder-led platform build).
the_case.detail: Every one of these engagements required the same core motion: understand what the client actually needs, configure the platform to meet it, and prove value fast. That's not a skill I'm claiming abstractly — it's the throughline of every recent role.

the_case.answer: Azure/Kubernetes at Eptura, Crocs, and Understood.org; API integrations at OVRSTCH (Stripe, WooPay, ApplePay, Printify); Salesforce CRM integration at Understood.org.
the_case.detail: The API integration work at OVRSTCH wasn't theoretical — Stripe, WooPay, ApplePay, and Printify all had to work together correctly for the business to actually function, with no engineering team behind me if something broke.

the_case.need: Project management across multiple simultaneous client implementations
the_case.answer: Understood.org: managed a distributed implementation team across 12+ countries. Eptura: coordinated implementation and adoption across ~10 concurrent teams.
the_case.detail: Multi-country, multi-team coordination has been a constant, not an exception — 12+ countries at Understood.org, ~10 concurrent teams spanning India and California at Eptura.

the_case.need: Translating complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders
the_case.answer: Primary technical point of contact for the VP of Engineering (Understood.org) and the Operations Board (Eptura) — consistently the bridge between engineering and business stakeholders.
the_case.detail: Executive-facing technical translation is the specific skill implementation work lives or dies on. I've been the named point of contact stakeholders went to when they needed the real answer, not the simplified one.

the_case.answer: Founder of OVRSTCH (built and configured an entire AI-native commerce platform solo); engagement lead at a stealth-mode AI startup navigating ambiguous, fast-moving requirements.
the_case.detail: OVRSTCH is direct proof — no team, no playbook, just building the thing and fixing what broke. That instinct transfers directly to client implementations where the requirements are still taking shape.

the_case.answer: Direct hands-on Salesforce CRM integration work at Understood.org (2019–2020), delivered alongside a full platform replatform.
the_case.detail: This is direct experience, not adjacency — I led the Salesforce integration work as part of a larger Sitecore-to-Contentful replatform, coordinating data integration requirements across a distributed team spanning 12+ countries.

the_case.need: Familiarity with intent-based and generative AI
the_case.answer: Daily practitioner of Claude Code, ChatGPT/GPT-4, and Google Gemini since 2022; led delivery for a startup building fine-tuned LLM models on these same platforms against proprietary datasets.
the_case.detail: Level AI's Auto-QA and Agent Assist products are solving the same class of problem I was implementing at the stealth-mode AI startup — extracting reliable signal from unstructured data using LLMs. This isn't a new domain for me.

the_stack.headline: The implementation toolkit.

when_things_break.scenario: A client implementation stalls because requirements were never fully understood before configuration began
response: At Understood.org, I inherited a distressed, multi-year program in its final crucial months
Led technical discovery to fully understand the CRM integration and replatform requirements before proceeding
Did not let configuration outrun understanding, even under deadline pressure
Result: delivered with zero critical regressions after taking over the program

when_things_break.scenario: Multiple concurrent client/team implementations start competing for the same limited technical resources
response: At Eptura, ~10 distributed teams spanning two continents all needed resourcing at once
Standardized cadences across every team so conflicts became visible early
Built executive dashboards so leadership could see resourcing conflicts before they became blockers
Result: 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency across the program

when_things_break.scenario: A solo implementation needs to integrate multiple third-party APIs with no dedicated engineering team
response: At OVRSTCH, I personally configured and integrated payment APIs (Stripe, WooPay, ApplePay) and automated fulfillment (Printify API)
Diagnosed a production bottleneck that was costing 25% of fulfillment cycle time
Re-sequenced the workflow myself, with no engineering team to hand it to

how_i_run_a_room: Every implementation starts with structured technical discovery — understanding the client's actual requirements before any configuration begins
Client-facing status updates translate technical progress into business terms before the client has to ask
RAID log maintained and reviewed at every client touchpoint; integration risks named before they become blockers
Configuration decisions are documented as they're made, not reconstructed after the fact
Retrospectives after each implementation feed directly into faster time-to-value on the next one
Upsell/cross-sell opportunities get flagged as they surface during discovery, not left for account management to find later

points: Led Salesforce CRM integration alongside a Sitecore-to-Contentful replatform

before: Client implementations stall in discovery before configuration ever begins
Technical requirements get lost in translation to the account team
Time-to-value slips past what was promised in the sales cycle
The client relationship weakens in the handoff from sales to delivery
after: A clear discovery-to-configuration process with no lost requirements
Technical decisions translated into business terms as they happen
Time-to-value that matches or beats what sales promised
A client relationship that survives — and strengthens through — the delivery handoff

the_ramp.deliverable: Review the current client implementation pipeline, shadow an active onboarding call, and read existing configuration documentation for the core Level AI products — Auto-QA, Analytics, Voice of the Customer, Agent Assist, Screen Recording.
Begin owning discovery and configuration for at least one active client account; identify the first process improvement opportunity in the current onboarding workflow.
Own end-to-end implementation for at least one active client account; establish working relationships with sales, product, and engineering counterparts.
